title: phelipanche orientalis
intro: Phelipanche orientalis, also known as the broomrape, is a parasitic plant species that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of Phelipanche orientalis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are typically prepared from the plant's roots and leaves.
